```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


#Load packages
```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(data.table) 
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)
library(vegan)
library(MuMIn)


```



#Data cleaning chunk 1. Here and in the next several data cleaning chunks we are getting rid of duplicate observations, unknown behaviors, and dealing with undirected behaviors
```{r}
matrix.please<-function(x) {
  m<-as.matrix(x[,-1])
  rownames(m)<-x[,1]
  m
}


`2022G1_interactions_raw` <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","2022_interactions_group1.csv"))
raw.allbehavedata <- `2022G1_interactions_raw`



birdIDs_capture <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","2022_birdIDs.csv")) 
birdIDs <- birdIDs_capture %>%
  dplyr::select(mark_id22) %>%
  filter(mark_id22!="") # all 20 birds




bird.list <- sort(unique(birdIDs$mark_id22)) # change each time
length(bird.list)
bird.list
length(bird.list)
# list of all possible dyads
dyad.list <- expand.grid(bird.list, bird.list) #head(dyad.list)
names(dyad.list) <- c("actor", "subject")
dyad.list <- subset(dyad.list, actor!=subject)
dyad.list$dyadID <- paste(dyad.list$actor, dyad.list$subject, sep="-")
# list of days with observations
obsday.list <- as.character(unique(raw.allbehavedata$date)) #only days with observations
obsday.list.wday <- as.data.frame(obsday.list)
colnames(obsday.list.wday) <- "date"
obsday.list.wday$nday <- seq(1:length(obsday.list.wday$date))

```
#Data cleaning chunk 2; "other" and "unknown" behaviors
```{r}
behave<-raw.allbehavedata 
unique(behave$behavior)
# other
behave.other<-filter(behave, behavior=="other") %>% dplyr::select(-behavior) %>%
  rename(behavior=other) %>%
  dplyr::select(orderkey, sessionKEY, session_start_timeStamp, date, full_time, time, actor, subject, behavior, everything())
behave.exl.other<-filter(behave, behavior!="other") %>% 
  dplyr::select(-other)
behave.goodother<-bind_rows(behave.exl.other, behave.other)
# unknown
behave.unknown <- filter(behave.goodother, behavior=="unknown") 
behave.unknown$behavior[behave.unknown$behavior=="unknown"] <-"unk"
behave.unknown <- behave.unknown %>%
  unite(behavior, behavior, unknown, sep = "_")
behave$behavior[behave$behavior=="unk agonistic"] <-"unk_agonistic"
behave.exl.unk<-behave.goodother %>% filter(behavior!="unknown") %>%
  dplyr::select(-unknown)
behave.goodunk<-bind_rows(behave.unknown, behave.exl.unk)
behave<-behave.goodunk
head(behave)




```
#Data cleaning chunk 3; prepare cleaned df
```{r}
# keep only observations where the actor is in the list of bird IDs
behave.goodID <- subset(behave, actor %in% bird.list)
# keep only observations where the actor (found above) and the subject are both in the list of bird IDs
behave.goodID <- subset(behave.goodID, subject %in% bird.list)
#finds n behaviors by sessionKEY, date, time, actor, subject, behavior
behave.key <- behave.goodID %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(sessionKEY, date, full_time,actor, subject, behavior) %>% 
  tally() # counts behaviors that were observed within the same minute
#finds max n behaviors per actor by date, time, and behavior type. Return just that summary
behave.maxkey<- behave.key %>% 
  group_by(actor, subject, date, full_time, behavior) %>%
  slice(which.max(n)) %>% # n.aggXaggXkey if summarise, n if tally
  ungroup()
head(behave.maxkey)
#finds n disps per actor by date for summarized/trimmed data
behaveXday <- behave.maxkey %>% 
  group_by(actor, subject, date, full_time, behavior) %>% 
  summarise(n=sum(n))
head(behaveXday)
min(behaveXday$n)





unique(behaveXday$behavior)
# behaviors in long st_unstat (1 event at a time)
behaveXday.long <- behaveXday %>%
  uncount(n) %>%
  mutate(n=1)
sum(behaveXday.long$n)
sum(behaveXday$n)
head(behaveXday.long)
## DUPLICATE UNDIRECTED BEHAVIORS
# filter the undirected behaviors 
behaveXday.undirected <- behaveXday.long %>% 
  filter(grepl(pattern="each other",tolower(behavior)))
  
behaveXday.undirected1 <-behaveXday.long %>%
  filter( behavior=="s2s") # spar as well?
behaveXday.undirected <- bind_rows(behaveXday.undirected,behaveXday.undirected1 )
# duplicate undirected dataframe and change subject and actor
behaveXday.undirected.dupl <- behaveXday.undirected %>%
  rename(subject=actor, actor=subject) %>%
  dplyr::select(actor, subject, everything())
# bind both dataframes
behaveXday.directed_2022G1.comb <- bind_rows(behaveXday.undirected, behaveXday.undirected.dupl) 
behaveXday.undir2dir <- behaveXday.directed_2022G1.comb %>%
  separate( behavior,into = c("behavior", "direction"), sep = "_") %>%
  dplyr::select(-direction)
unique(behaveXday.undir2dir$behavior)
## COMBINE DATAFRAMES
# bind to directed dataframe
behaveXday.directed_2022G1 <- behaveXday.long %>% 
  filter(!grepl(pattern="each other",tolower(behavior)) & behavior!="s2s")
unique(behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior)
behaveXday.directed_2022G1 <- bind_rows(behaveXday.directed_2022G1, behaveXday.undir2dir)
behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior[behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior=="NN"] <- "nn"
behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior[behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior=="alloP"] <- "allop"
behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior[behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior=="allopreen"] <- "allop"
length(behaveXday.directed_2022G1$actor)
unique(behaveXday.directed_2022G1$behavior)
12454 + 2587 # 15041
head(behaveXday.directed_2022G1)



```
#Add obs session to df
```{r}
#add obs session
head(behaveXday.directed_2022G1)

behaveXday.directed_2022G1 <- ungroup(behaveXday.directed_2022G1)
behaveXday.directed_2022G1 <- arrange(behaveXday.directed_2022G1, date, full_time)
#procedure to add obs session

#store the first row as a separate df. It will be binded back on later 
row_1_obs <- behaveXday.directed_2022G1[1,]

#put events side by side. This will allow us to subtract consecutive times from one another to determine whether an event took place on a separate observation session. 
obs_period_pt_1 <- mutate(behaveXday.directed_2022G1, actor_2 = lead(actor),
                          subject_2 = lead(subject),                                   
                          full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                          date2 = lead(date),
                          behavior_2 = lead (behavior)
                          )




#add empty columns for observation period and date period
obs_period_pt_1$obs_period <- NA
obs_period_pt_1$date_period <- NA

q <- 1

#loop to calculate observation sessions. If consecutive events were on the same day but there was a long time between events, it is determined that the 2nd event took place in a separate observation session. The ticker goes up, and is reset if events took place on different days.changed time from 30 min to 35 minutes cuz one instance where 30 min just added an obs session by a bit
for (i in 1:nrow(obs_period_pt_1)) {ifelse(obs_period_pt_1$date[i] == obs_period_pt_1$date2[i]
                                           & obs_period_pt_1$full_time_2[i] - obs_period_pt_1$full_time[i] >= 2100, q <- q+1, q <- q)
  ifelse(obs_period_pt_1$date[i] != obs_period_pt_1$date2[i], q <- 1, q <- q)
  obs_period_pt_1$obs_period [i] <- q
}

#remove original columns and keep mutated columns. These will replace original columns
obs_period_pt_2 <- subset(obs_period_pt_1, select = c(date2,  full_time_2, actor_2,  subject_2, behavior_2, obs_period, date_period  ))

#get rid of the bottom, which will be full of NAs, as the last event in the original data does not have anything that comes after
obs_period_pt_3 <- slice(obs_period_pt_2, 1:(n() - 1))


#rename mutated columns with original names 
setnames(obs_period_pt_3, old = c('date2',  'full_time_2', 'actor_2',  'subject_2','behavior_2'), 
         new = c('date',  'full_time', 'actor',  'subject', 'behavior'))

#add observation period "1" onto first row that we subseted off, as the first event in the data has to take place in obs session 1
row_1_obs$obs_period <- 1 
row_1_obs$date_period <- NA

#ensure that row 1 matches the df
row_1_obs_ready <-  subset(row_1_obs, select = c(date,  full_time, actor,  subject, behavior, obs_period, date_period  ))

#bind first row and df together
obs_period_pt_4 <- rbind(row_1_obs_ready, obs_period_pt_3)
obs_period_pt_4$date_period <- paste(obs_period_pt_4$date,obs_period_pt_4$obs_period)




head(obs_period_pt_4)
```





#bind bin number and stability period onto raw data



#day level bind bin number and stability period onto raw data
```{r}
three_day_bins_2022G1 <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","2022_3daybins.csv"))
MOPA_power_score_2022G1 <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","MOPA_date_rank_2022G1.csv "))

head(three_day_bins_2022G1)
three_day_bins_2022G1$date <- as.Date(three_day_bins_2022G1$date, format = "%m/%d/%Y")

#filter for study period
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- three_day_bins_2022G1[(three_day_bins_2022G1$date >= "2022-1-26" & three_day_bins_2022G1$date <= "2022-05-11" ),]

three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- three_day_bins_2022G1_sub %>% rename(type = experiment)

three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- three_day_bins_2022G1_sub %>% filter(type != "na")


#subset for only columns of interest and replace nas with appropriate bin number and type with appropriate type  (here its the character na)
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- subset(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub, select = c("date", "type", "bin")) %>%
  mutate(binpt2 = lead(bin), typept2 = lead(type)) %>%
  mutate(type = ifelse(bin == "na", typept2, type ),
         bin = ifelse(bin == "na", binpt2, bin )
         )


#remove integers from social stability type
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub$type <- gsub("[0-30]+", "", three_day_bins_2022G1_sub$type)

#subset for columns of interest
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- subset(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub, select = c("date", "type", "bin"))

three_day_bins_2022G1_sub$bin <- as.numeric(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub$bin)

#add on column for stable and unstable periods
#figure out the phase for each bin/type combo.
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- three_day_bins_2022G1_sub %>%
  distinct(type, bin, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  #Sort the data by bin so that theyre processed in order to effectively  detect changes in phase
  arrange(bin) %>%
  #detect previous rows type and return true when type changes. Turns that into a grouping variable so every time type changes the group number increases by 1
  mutate(type_change = type != lag(type, default = first(type)),
         type_group = cumsum(type_change)) %>%
  group_by(type_group) %>%
  #for each group of consecutive bins of the same type label, make the first row unstable and the following row stable
  mutate(phase = ifelse(row_number() == 1, "unstable", "stable")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(bin, type, phase) %>%
  #bind back to original dataset
  right_join(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub, by = c("bin", "type"))

#convert bin to numeric for sorting
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub$bin <- as.numeric(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub$bin)
three_day_bins_2022G1_sub <- arrange(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub,bin)

head(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub)
#subset for columns of interest
MOPA_power_score_2022G1_sub <- subset(MOPA_power_score_2022G1, select = c("date", "id", "rank"))


#filter main df so that df only contains crowds and displacements
aggresion_sub<- filter(obs_period_pt_4, behavior %in% c("crowd","displace", "chase"))



#filter for study period
aggresion_sub <- aggresion_sub[(aggresion_sub$date >= "2022-01-26" & aggresion_sub$date <= "2022-05-11" ),]


head(MOPA_power_score_2022G1)
MOPA_power_score_2022G1_sub <- subset(MOPA_power_score_2022G1, select = c("date", "id", "rank"))


#bind phase df and rank df onto main aggression df
aggresion_sub_pt2 <- aggresion_sub %>%
  left_join(three_day_bins_2022G1_sub, by = c("date")) %>%
  rename(bin=bin) %>%
  arrange(date, full_time) %>%
  left_join(MOPA_power_score_2022G1_sub, by = c("date", "actor"= "id")) %>%
  rename(actor_rank = rank) %>%
  left_join(MOPA_power_score_2022G1_sub, by = c("date", "subject"= "id")) %>%
  rename(subject_rank = rank)


#based on the way we filtered were going to manually set the first bin (bin2) to stable
aggresion_sub_pt2$phase[aggresion_sub_pt2$bin == 2] <- "stable"

```



```{r}
#Prepare/order & give each row an id + numeric time
df <- aggresion_sub_pt2 %>%
  arrange(date_period, date, full_time) %>%
  mutate(.row = row_number(),
         tsec = as.numeric(full_time))  # seconds since midnight

#Previous-by-actor (used for  base A to B within 60s check) 
by_actor <- df %>%
  group_by(date_period, actor) %>%
  mutate(prev_subj_by_actor = lag(subject),
         prev_tsec_by_actor = lag(tsec)) %>%
  ungroup()

#Last involvement time for each participant (actor OR subject) 
# Build a long view with one entry per participant per event
long <- bind_rows(
  by_actor %>% transmute(date_period, .row, participant = actor, tsec),
  by_actor %>% transmute(date_period, .row, participant = subject, tsec)
) %>%
  arrange(date_period, tsec, .row) %>%
  group_by(date_period, participant) %>%
  mutate(prev_involve_tsec = lag(tsec)) %>%
  ungroup()

#For each original row, get the most recent involvement of either A or B before this row
last_involve <- long %>%
  group_by(date_period, .row) %>%
  summarise(last_involve_tsec = max(prev_involve_tsec, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(last_involve_tsec = ifelse(is.finite(last_involve_tsec), last_involve_tsec, NA_real_))

#Apply  rule of getting rid of consectuive 60 sec possible chases
aggression_no_repeat_custom <- by_actor %>%
  left_join(last_involve, by = c("date_period", ".row")) %>%
  mutate(
    #Base repeat condition. previous by same actor is the same subject and within 60s
    within_actor_repeat = !is.na(prev_tsec_by_actor) &
                          subject == prev_subj_by_actor &
                          (tsec - prev_tsec_by_actor) <= 60,

    #Detect if there was an intervening event involving A or B after that previous A to B
    interrupted_by_A_or_B = within_actor_repeat &
                            !is.na(last_involve_tsec) &
                            (last_involve_tsec > prev_tsec_by_actor),

    keep = !within_actor_repeat | interrupted_by_A_or_B
  ) %>%
  filter(keep) %>%
dplyr::select(-.row, -tsec,
         -prev_subj_by_actor, -prev_tsec_by_actor,
         -last_involve_tsec, -within_actor_repeat, -interrupted_by_A_or_B, -keep)


obs <- aggression_no_repeat_custom$date_period



dater <- unique(obs)
datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
actorer <- unique(aggression_no_repeat_custom$actor)
actex <- c(1:length(actorer))




#sort by time and date
pass_filt_binded_2022G1 <- aggression_no_repeat_custom %>%
  arrange(aggression_no_repeat_custom$date, aggression_no_repeat_custom$full_time)


colSums(is.na(pass_filt_binded_2022G1))

#bottom rows of df are full of NA rows from where bird was removed or didnt aggressively interact. Checked for NAs throughout rest of df. clean through rest so removing final NA rows 
#pass_filt_binded_2022G1 <- na.omit(pass_filt_binded_2022G1)
#to here





pass_aggression_list_2022G1_again <- list()
#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird
for(i in datex) { 
      for (g in actex){
    output <- filter(pass_filt_binded_2022G1, pass_filt_binded_2022G1$date_period == dater[i] & (subject == actorer[g] | actor == actorer[g]))
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
        output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
    pass_aggression_list_2022G1_again[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output}

}
 
#ensure df arranged by date and time
pass_aggression_list_2022G1_again <- lapply(pass_aggression_list_2022G1_again, function(x) arrange(x, date, full_time))





#modified data frame to put consecutive events side by side

pass_side_by_side <- lapply(pass_aggression_list_2022G1_again, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor),
                                                                          actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                        subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                        full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                        date2 = lead(date))) 


```

#Calculate time threshold
```{r}
dateact1 <-  c(1:length(pass_side_by_side))

#rename df for calculation of time to pass in seconds
pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1 <- pass_side_by_side


#pick out pass along rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
for(i in dateact1 ) {
  pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1[[i]] <- mutate(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject == actor_2 
                                                                 & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                                                 & date2 == date
                                                                 & subject == counter
                                                                 & actor_2 == counter
                                                                 & actor_2_rank < subject_2_rank
                                                                 & actor != subject_2,
                                                                 "yes","no"), time_to_pass = full_time_2 - full_time)
  
}




 #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
 pass_time_huge_2022G1 <- bind_rows(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1, .id = "column_label")
 
 
 

#get df where rule was followed
pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1 <- subset(pass_time_huge_2022G1, rule_followed != "no")
 
 #subset for columns of interest
 pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank,
                                                                         actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank, full_time_2, date2, counter, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase ))
 

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1$time_to_pass)
 

ggplot(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1,    aes(x = bin, y = time_to_pass, group = bin, colour = phase)) + geom_boxplot() +
     coord_cartesian( ylim = c(0,75))


ggplot(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1,    aes(x = bin, y = time_to_pass, group = bin, colour = type)) + geom_boxplot() +
     coord_cartesian( ylim = c(0,75))







```



#calculate number of rule follows
```{r}
#read in previously calculated thresholds (group 1 and 2)
summary.pass_length_all <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","summary.full_pass_length_st_unst_all.csv"))

#round threshold to whole number  
summary.pass_length_all$q95 <- round(summary.pass_length_all$q95)

# Merge both in and out variables onto the full actor-subject pair list

pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh <- pass_filt_binded_2022G1 %>%
  left_join(summary.pass_length_all, by = c("bin" = "bin2")) 


#pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh <- subset(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh, select = -c(...1))



pass_aggression_list_thresh_2022G1 <- list()
dater <- unique(obs)
datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
actorer <- unique(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh$actor)
actex <- c(1:length(actorer))



#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird
for(i in datex) { 
      for (g in actex){
    output <- filter(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh, pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh$date_period == dater[i] & (subject == actorer[g] | actor == actorer[g]))
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
        output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
    pass_aggression_list_thresh_2022G1[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output}

}






#modified data frame to put consecutive events side by side

pass_side_by_side_thresh <- lapply(pass_aggression_list_thresh_2022G1, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor),
                                                                          actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                        subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                        full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                        date2 = lead(date))) 




pass_side_by_side_for_perm_2022G1 <- pass_side_by_side_thresh





dateact1 <-  c(1:length(pass_side_by_side_thresh))


#pick out pass along rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
for(i in dateact1 ) {
  pass_side_by_side_thresh[[i]] <- mutate(pass_side_by_side_thresh[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject == actor_2 
                                                                 & full_time_2 - full_time <= q95 
                                                                 & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                                                 & date2 == date
                                                                 & subject == counter
                                                                 & actor_2 == counter
                                                                 & actor_2_rank < subject_2_rank
                                                                 & actor != subject_2,
                                                                 "yes","no"), time_to_pass = full_time_2 - full_time)
} 




#combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
pass_huge <- bind_rows(pass_side_by_side_thresh, .id = "column_label")

#sort by time and date
pass_huge_sorted <- pass_huge %>%
  arrange(pass_huge$date, pass_huge$full_time)


#need to check but i think it works to get rid of all "initiated no" rows to get rid of duplicates, as pass along 
#is literally possible whenever an aggressive event takes place

pass_huge_sorted_no_dupr <- subset(pass_huge_sorted, initiated != "no") 

pass_no_dupr_2022G1 <- subset(pass_huge_sorted_no_dupr, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, bin, behavior, type, phase, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank,  full_time_2, date2, counter, initiated, rule_followed,time_to_pass))

table(pass_no_dupr_2022G1$rule_followed)

pass_real_2022G1 <- length(which(pass_no_dupr_2022G1$rule_followed == "yes"))





pass_doner_all_yes_2022G1 <-filter(pass_no_dupr_2022G1, rule_followed == "yes")



```



# individual rule following actor


```{r}

#test for individual use
unique(pass_doner_all_yes_2022G1$actor_2)

summary.pass.individ_2022G1 <- pass_doner_all_yes_2022G1 %>% group_by(date, phase, actor_2) %>%
  summarize (
    count =n())

summary.pass.individ_2022G1




# Plot
individ_pass_plot_2022G1 <- ggplot(summary.pass.individ_2022G1, aes(x = actor_2, y = count, colour = phase  )) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) + 
  facet_wrap(~ date) +
  theme_minimal()  +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust=0.5),
    axis.title.x = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(t = 15)),  
    axis.title.y = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(r = 15)),
    ) +
  labs(
    x = "Parakeet ID",  
    y = "Count of rule follows"  
  )

individ_pass_plot_2022G1




summary.pass.individ_2022G1 <- pass_doner_all_yes_2022G1 %>% group_by(date, bin, phase, type, actor_2) %>%
  summarize (
    count =n())

summary.pass.individ_2022G1



# Plot
individ_pass_plot_2022G1 <- ggplot(summary.pass.individ_2022G1, aes(x = actor_2, y = count, colour = type  )) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) + 
  facet_wrap(~ date) +
  theme_minimal()  +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust=0.5),
    axis.title.x = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(t = 15)),  
    axis.title.y = element_text( face = "bold",  margin = margin(r = 15)),
    ) +
  labs(
    x = "Parakeet ID",  
    y = "Count of rule follows"  
  )

individ_pass_plot_2022G1

head(summary.pass.individ_2022G1)






#metrics per date 
metrics_pass_2022G1_actor <- summary.pass.individ_2022G1 %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    Richness  = sum(count > 0, na.rm = TRUE),                    
    Diversity = if (sum(count, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) 0 else vegan::diversity(count, index = "shannon"),
    Evenness  = dplyr::if_else(Richness > 1, Diversity / log(Richness), NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary.pass.individ_2022G1_no_act_count <- subset(summary.pass.individ_2022G1, select = -c(actor_2, count))
#keep only distinct rows 
summary.pass.individ_2022G1_no_act_count <- distinct(summary.pass.individ_2022G1_no_act_count)


#bind bin phase and type onto new metrics summary df
metrics_pass_2022G1_actor <- metrics_pass_2022G1_actor  %>% 
  left_join(summary.pass.individ_2022G1_no_act_count, by = "date")





#group size denominator = unique individuals in each group across both roles so proportional richness is comparable across groups)
#determine how many birds were in the experiment and did anything at each date
head(obs_period_pt_4)
df_4_group_size <- obs_period_pt_4[(obs_period_pt_4$date >= "2022-01-26" & obs_period_pt_4$date <= "2022-05-11" ),]



group_sizes <- df_4_group_size %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(N = n_distinct(c(actor, subject), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")


#add in rule type 
metrics_pass_2022G1_actor$rule <- "Pass-along"

#calculate proportional richness 
metrics_pass_2022G1_actor <- metrics_pass_2022G1_actor %>%
  left_join(group_sizes, by = "date") %>%
  mutate(
    Proportional_Richness = if_else(
      rule %in% c("Retaliation","Pile-on"),
      Richness / N,
      Richness / pmax(N - 1, 1)  # avoid divide-by-zero
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(date, rule)

metrics_pass_2022G1_actor

#write.csv(metrics_pass_2022G1_actor,  here("ch3 data","metrics_pass_2022G1_actor.csv"), row.names = FALSE)






ggplot(data = metrics_pass_2022G1_actor, aes(x = date, y = Diversity)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = type, size = 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 3)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Diversity Across dates",
       x = "date", y = "Value") 




ggplot(data = metrics_pass_2022G1_actor, aes(x = date, y = Evenness)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = type, size = 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Evenness Across dates",
       x = "date", y = "Value") 




ggplot(data = metrics_pass_2022G1_actor, aes(x = date, y = Proportional_Richness)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = type, size = 1)) +
 coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Proportional_Richness Across dates",
       x = "date", y = "Value")
```

#randomize order of events
```{r}

#REMEMBER TO BIND THRESHOLD TO DF
start_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")



pass_obs_bin_2022G1 <- unique(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh$bin)
pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1 <- list()

pbouter = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = length(pass_obs_bin_2022G1), style = 3)

set.seed(249)

for(k in pass_obs_bin_2022G1) { 
  runouter <- k
  setTxtProgressBar(pbouter, k) #update progress bar
  loop.run_outer <- paste("outer run", runouter) 
  loop.run_outer
  
  pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand <- subset(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_thresh, bin == k )  
  
  
  #remove extra columns that create spam in loop
  pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand <- subset(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, bin, behavior, obs_period, date_period, type, phase, actor_rank, subject_rank, counter, q95 ))
  
  
  date_x <- pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand$date
  obs <- pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand$date_period
  #separate data into a list with each df=a date so we can randomize within days
  #sort_by_date <- function(x, i, y) {y <- filter(x, date == i )
  #mutate(y, date_counter = i)}
  
  datelist <- list()
  dater <- unique(obs)
  datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
  #boop <- filter(both_numbered_sorted, both_numbered_sorted$date == datex [2])
  
  #loop to create a separate dataframe for each bird that only contains interactions #pertaining to that bird.
  #Each dataframe is stored within a list to make the dataframes easy to work with
  #separate data into list of each obs session each day 
  for(i in datex) { 
    output <- filter(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand, pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand$date_period == dater[i] )
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
    datelist[[paste0("list", dater[i])]] <- output}
  
  
  
  
  #run ids should be 1 through however many times you want to run through the permutation
  runIDs <- seq(1,1000)
  
  #create an empty masterframe to be filled with each run of the permutation
  masterframe_pass_2022G1.st_unst.df <- data.frame(runIDs = character(),
                                                 actor = character(),
                                                 actor_rank = numeric(),
                                                 subject = character(),
                                                 subject_rank = numeric(),
                                                 date = character(),
                                                 full_time = integer(),
                                                 bin = integer(),
                                                 behavior = character(),
                                                 type = character(),
                                                 phase = character(),
                                                 actor_2 = character(),
                                                 actor_2_rank = numeric(),
                                                 subject_2 = character(),
                                                 subject_2_rank = numeric(),
                                                 full_time_2 = integer(),
                                                 date2 = character(),
                                                 q95 = numeric(),
                                                 counter = character(),
                                                 initiated = character(),
                                                 rule_followed = character(),
                                                 stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  nruns <- 1000
  #pb = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = nruns, style = 3)
  
  #forloop starts here'
  for (r in 1:length(runIDs)) { #r = 1 is a test you can uncomment to see if it works before #running it 100 times
    #r = 1
    run <- r
    #setTxtProgressBar(pb, r) #update progress bar
    loop.run <- paste("inner run", run) 
    loop.run
    #for example, if r = 1, looprun will say run 1 which will allow you to keep each run's #data straight
    
    
    #loop.seed <- set.seed[r] didnt work so ignore. not needed
    
    
    #renamed data
    loop.datalist <- datelist
    xorder <- c()
    listlength <- as.numeric(length(loop.datalist))
    datex2 <- 1:listlength
    full_time_list <-c()
    loop.data.randomized.time.added <- c()
    #get rid of anything thats not actor and receiver (we will bind back on the date and time #later anyways to preserve
    #their order)
    
    #remove date from each df in the list
    #create a randomized sequence of numbers to bind to the subset data (ex. 2,6,1,3,5,4)
    #sort the data frame to put the randomized numbers in order from 1 to whatever and voila, #the order of events has been randomized (ex. from above 1,2,3,4,5,6)
    
    for(i in datex2) {
      full_time_list[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = c(full_time))
      loop.datalist[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = -c(full_time))
      
      xorder[[i]] <- data.frame(xorder = sample(1:length(loop.datalist[[i]]$actor),
                                                length(loop.datalist[[i]]$actor)))
      loop.datalist[[i]] <- mutate(loop.datalist[[i]], 
                                   xorderx = xorder[[i]]$xorder)
      loop.datalist[[i]] <-
        loop.datalist[[i]][order(loop.datalist[[i]]$xorderx),]
      #bind time back on
      loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]] <- bind_cols(loop.datalist[[i]], full_time_list[[i]])
      loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]] <- subset(loop.data.randomized.time.added[[i]], select = -c(xorderx))
      
    }
    
    practdrlist <- loop.data.randomized.time.added
    
    

#separate data into list of each obs session each day for each bird

actex <- c(1:length(actorer))
dater <- unique(obs)
practdrlist_split_by_bird <- c()

for(i in datex2) {
for (g in actex) {output <- subset(practdrlist[[i]], actor == actorer[g] |  subject == actorer[g])
#if one of the dfs in the list is empty, add NA
   if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
  output <- mutate(output, counter = actorer[g])
  practdrlist_split_by_bird[[paste0("list", dater[i], actorer[g])]] <- output
}}
    
    
    pass_2022G1_rand_split_by_bird_ordered <- lapply(practdrlist_split_by_bird, function(x) arrange(x, date, full_time))
    
    
    
    
    #modified each data frame to put consecutive events side by side
    
    pass_rand_test_2022G1 <- lapply(pass_2022G1_rand_split_by_bird_ordered, function(x) mutate(x, actor_2 = lead(actor), 
                                                                                   actor_2_rank = lead(actor_rank), 
                                                                                   subject_2 = lead(subject), 
                                                                                   subject_2_rank = lead(subject_rank), 
                                                                                   full_time_2 = lead(full_time), 
                                                                                   date2 = lead(date))) 
    
    
    
    
    #successfully picked out pass rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
    #loop to set conditionals to pick out pass
    
    #counter corresponds with which birds dataframe I am looking at and is used to control for false positives 
    #for example, if pass is detected for a bird whos specific dataframe I am not looking #at , this may be a false positive, as that birds interactions are not all listed in the #dataframe of another bird. In other words, the bird may have done something else to another #bird that is not listed, as it would be only listed in that specific birds dataframe
    dateact2 <-  c(1:length(pass_rand_test_2022G1))
    
    
    
    
    for(i in dateact2) {
      pass_rand_test_2022G1[[i]] <- mutate(pass_rand_test_2022G1[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject == actor_2 
                                                                 & full_time_2 - full_time <= q95 
                                                                 & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                                                 & date2 == date
                                                                 & subject == counter
                                                                 & actor_2 == counter
                                                                 & actor_2_rank < subject_2_rank
                                                                 & actor != subject_2,
                                                                 "yes","no"), time_to_pass = full_time_2 - full_time)
    } 
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
    testhuge <- bind_rows(pass_rand_test_2022G1, .id = "column_label")
    
    #sort by time and date
    testhugesorted <- testhuge %>%
      arrange(testhuge$date, testhuge$full_time)
    
    
    
    #need to check but i think it works to get rid of all "initiated no" rows to get rid of #duplicates, as pass 
    #is literally possible whenever an aggressive event takes place
    #Update. It does work
    
    testhugesorted_no_dup <- subset(testhugesorted, initiated != "no") 
    
    pass_no_dup_rand_2022G1 <- subset(testhugesorted_no_dup, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, bin, behavior, type, phase, counter, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank,  full_time_2, date2, q95, initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass))
    
    
    
    
    
    
    #end of pass process
    #end of rule code
    
    
    
    #bind vector of run IDs for each run to the dataframe so that you can have a numeric run #id next to each data point
    #that coresponds with that run which will allow you to sort/put them in ascending order later
    runIDs <- rep(loop.run, length(pass_no_dup_rand_2022G1$actor))
    
    pass_complete <- cbind(runIDs, pass_no_dup_rand_2022G1)
    
    
    #put each runs data into teh masterframe
    masterframe_pass_2022G1.st_unst.df <- rbind(masterframe_pass_2022G1.st_unst.df, pass_complete)
    
  }
  pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1[[paste0("bin", k)]] <- masterframe_pass_2022G1.st_unst.df
}

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded <-  bind_rows(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1, .id = "column_label")


end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")
start_time
end_time




#write.csv(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded,  here("ch3 data","pass_countmaster_stable_unstable_2022G1_custom_thresh.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}


#pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","pass_countmaster_stable_unstable_2022G1_custom_thresh.csv"))


head(pass_doner_all_yes_2022G1) 



pass_count_length_sum_2022G1 <- pass_doner_all_yes_2022G1 %>% group_by( bin) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (
    count =n())

pass_count_length_sum_2022G1


```

#bin1
```{r}
#bin1

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin1 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 1)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin1_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 1)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin1$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin1$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin1$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin1 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin1_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin1

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin1 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin1_real$count > rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin1 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin1/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin1
 # 0 right
st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin1.df <- data.frame(bin = 1, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin1  )
```


#bin2
```{r}
#bin2

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin2 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 2)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin2_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 2)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin2$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin2$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin2$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin2 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin2_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin2

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin2 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin2_real$count > rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin2 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin2/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin2
 # 0 left





#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin2 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin2_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin2_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin2 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 2,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin2,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "before",
  rank_4_treatment = "none",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "high",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "removal1", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin2 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin2, new_row)


st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin2.df <- data.frame(bin = 2, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin2  )
```

```{r}
#bin3

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin3 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 3)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin3 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin3_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin3
 
st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin3 <-length(which(  pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin3_real$count < rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin3 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin3/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin3
 # 0.002 left



#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin3 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin3_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin3_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin3 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 3,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0.002,
  z_score = z_score_bin3,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "high",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "removal1", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin3 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin3, new_row)



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin3.df <- data.frame(bin = 3, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin3  )
```

```{r}
# Calculate z-score
```

#bin6
```{r}
#bin6

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin6 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 6)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin6_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 6)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin6$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin6$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin6$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin6 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin6_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin6

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin6 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin6_real$count >rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin6 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin6/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin6
 #p = 0 left


#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin6 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin6_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin6_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin6 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 6,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin6,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "reintroduction",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "middle",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "removal2", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin6 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin6, new_row)




st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin6.df <- data.frame(bin = 6, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin6  )
```



#bin7
```{r}
#bin7

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin7 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 7)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin7_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 7)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin7$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin7$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin7$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin7 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin7_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin7

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin7 <-length(which( pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin7_real$count < rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin7 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin7/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
# 0left
st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin7





#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin7 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin7_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin7_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin7 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 7,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin7,
  smls = "faster",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "middle",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "middle",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "removal2", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin7 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin7, new_row)


 
st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin7.df <- data.frame(bin = 7, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin7  )
```

```{r}
#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin11 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin11_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin11

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin11 <-length(which( pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin11_real$count >rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin11 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin11/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin11
 #0 left


#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin11 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin11_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin11_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin11 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 11,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin11,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "middle",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "middle",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "removal3", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin11 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin11, new_row)




st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin11.df <- data.frame(bin = 11, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin11  )
```



#bin12
```{r}
#bin12

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin12 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 12)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin12_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 12)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin12$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin12$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin12$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin12 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin12_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin12

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin12 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin12_real$count > rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin12 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin12/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin12
 # 0 left


#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin12 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin12_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin12_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin12 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 12,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin12,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "middle",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "middle",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "reintroduction3", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin12 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin12, new_row)




st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin12.df <- data.frame(bin = 12, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin12  )
```

```{r}
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin16

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin16 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin16_real$count > rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin16 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin16/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin16
 # 0 left

#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin16 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin16_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin16_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin16 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 16,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin16,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "high",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "reintroduction4", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin16 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin16, new_row)


st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin16.df <- data.frame(bin = 16, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin16  )
```


#bin17
```{r}
#bin17

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin17 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 17)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin17_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 17)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin17$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin17$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin17$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin17 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin17_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin17

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin17 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin17_real$count > rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin17 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin17/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin17
 # 0 left


#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin17 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin17_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin17_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin17 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 17,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin17,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "reintroduction",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "high",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "reintroduction4", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin17 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin17, new_row)



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin17.df <- data.frame(bin = 17, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin17  )
```

```{r}
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 21,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin21,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "reintroduction",
  rank_4_treatment = "low",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "low",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "reintroduction5", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin21 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin21, new_row)



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin21.df <- data.frame(bin = 21, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin21  )
```


#bin22
```{r}
#bin22

pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin22 <- subset(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded, bin == 22)

pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin22_real <- subset(pass_count_length_sum_2022G1, bin == 22)




#put run #s in an ascending vector
cum_run_IDS <- unique(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin22$runIDs)

#create empty vector for number of time the rules were followed to be stored
random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c()


#put the number of times the rule was followed into the vector for analysis 
for (j in cum_run_IDS) {
  len_of_passs <- length(which(pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin22$rule_followed == "yes" & 
                                       pass_count_length_list_masterframe_2022G1_binded_bin22$runIDs == j))
  
  random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 <- c(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1, len_of_passs)
}

#turn vector into a dataframe for analysis
rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1 <- data.frame(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

#plot time (the x intercept is the # of time a rule was used in the real data, found with 
#"pass_real <- length(which(pass_no_dupr$rule_followed == "yes"))
#pass_real.frame <- data.frame(pass_real))"
st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin22 <- ggplot(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1, aes(x=random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=5,
                 colour="black", fill="grey") +
  geom_density(lwd = 1, linetype = 1) + theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin22_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0,.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = seq(0, .05, .025)) 

st_unst_pass_2022G1_count_plot_bin22

st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin22 <-length(which(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin22_real$count < rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1 ))



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin22 <-   1 - st_unst_pass_tally2022G1_bin22/length(rand_pass_.frame_st_unst_2022G1$random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin22
 # 0 left


#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts
mean_perm <- mean(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)
sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin22 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin22_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin22_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later
st_unst_df_piece_bin22 <- data.frame(group = integer(),
                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),
                                    z_score= integer(),
                                    smls = character(),
                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment = character(),
                                    rank_4_treatment_pp = character(),
                                    pre_post = character(),
                                    disturbanceID = character()
                                    )

#input data to be combined into df
new_row <- data.frame(
  group = 2,
  bin = 22,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin22,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "stable",
  treatment = "reintroduction",
  rank_4_treatment = "low",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "high",
  pre_post = "pre",
  disturbanceID = "removal6", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin22 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin22, new_row)



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin22.df <- data.frame(bin = 22, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin22  )
```

```{r}
  geom_vline(xintercept = pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin23_real$count, colour = "red", size = 2)  + theme(axis.text = element_text(size=25),  axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Count of pass events", breaks=seq(0, 200, 50)) +

 # 0 left




#calculate z score
#calculate mean and SD of permuted counts

sd_perm <- sd(random_times_pass_followed_st_unst_2022G1)

# Calculate z-score
z_score_bin23 <- with(pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin23_real, ifelse(sd_perm == 0, NA, (pass_count_length_list_2022G1_bin23_real$count - mean_perm) / sd_perm))

#create df to store important data for later stats and figures. to be binded later

                                    bin = integer(),
                                    rule = character(),
                                    cmls = character(),
                                    p_cmls = integer(),

                                    stability = character(),
                                    treatment = character(),

                                    )

#input data to be combined into df

  group = 2,
  bin = 23,
  rule = "pass",
  cmls = "less",
  p_cmls =  0,
  z_score = z_score_bin23,
  smls = "same",
  stability = "unstable",
  treatment = "removal",
  rank_4_treatment = "high",
  rank_4_treatment_pp = "high",
  pre_post = "post",
  disturbanceID = "removal6", 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
#bind row of data to df
st_unst_df_piece_bin23 <- rbind(st_unst_df_piece_bin23, new_row)



st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin23.df <- data.frame(bin = 23, p_value = st_unst_ppass_tally2022G1_bin23  )
```
