#combine to make piece of masterframe
```{r}
#bind all summary dfs to make group masterframe
st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_pass_2022G1 <- rbind(  st_unst_df_piece_bin2, st_unst_df_piece_bin3, st_unst_df_piece_bin4, st_unst_df_piece_bin5, st_unst_df_piece_bin6, st_unst_df_piece_bin7, st_unst_df_piece_bin8, st_unst_df_piece_bin9, st_unst_df_piece_bin10, st_unst_df_piece_bin11, st_unst_df_piece_bin12, st_unst_df_piece_bin13, st_unst_df_piece_bin14, st_unst_df_piece_bin15, st_unst_df_piece_bin16, st_unst_df_piece_bin17, st_unst_df_piece_bin18, st_unst_df_piece_bin19, st_unst_df_piece_bin20, st_unst_df_piece_bin21, st_unst_df_piece_bin22, st_unst_df_piece_bin23, st_unst_df_piece_bin24, st_unst_df_piece_bin25, st_unst_df_piece_bin26)


#write.csv(st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_pass_2022G1,  here("ch3 data","st_unst_df_piece_master_piece_pass_2022G1.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```


#randomize rule speed
```{r}
start_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")



dateact1 <-  c(1:length(pass_side_by_side))

#rename df for calculation of time to pass in seconds
pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1 <- pass_side_by_side


#pick out pass along rule while keeping all dataframes in a list (yay!!!)
for(i in dateact1 ) {
  pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1[[i]] <- mutate(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1[[i]], 
                                           initiated = ifelse(subject == counter, "yes","no"), 
                                         rule_followed = ifelse( subject == actor_2 
                                                                 & full_time_2 - full_time > 0
                                                                 & date2 == date
                                                                 & subject == counter
                                                                 & actor_2 == counter
                                                                 & actor_2_rank < subject_2_rank
                                                                 & actor != subject_2,
                                                                 "yes","no"), time_to_pass = full_time_2 - full_time)
}




 #combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
 pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked <- bind_rows(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1, .id = "column_label")
 
 

#Remove duplicate and double counted sequences
pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked <- distinct(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked, date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank, obs_period, date_period, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank, full_time_2, date2, time_to_pass, bin, behavior, type, phase,  .keep_all= TRUE) 







#remove instances of rule use whos speed were calculated to be 0. In this case we cant be certain of which event actually came first
pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked <- filter(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked, time_to_pass != 0)



pass_obs_bin_2022G1 <- unique(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked$bin)
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_2022G1 <- list()

pbouter = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = length(pass_obs_bin_2022G1), style = 3)

set.seed(300)

for(k in pass_obs_bin_2022G1) { 
  runouter <- k
  setTxtProgressBar(pbouter, k) #update progress bar
  loop.run_outer <- paste("outer run", runouter) 
  loop.run_outer
  
  pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand <- subset(pass_side_by_side_time_2022G1_marked, bin == k )  
  
  
  date_x <- pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand$date
  obs <- pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand$date_period
  #separate data into a list with each df=a date so we can randomize within days
  #sort_by_date <- function(x, i, y) {y <- filter(x, date == i )
  #mutate(y, date_counter = i)}
  
  pass_side_by_side_tested_time_2022G1_list <- list()
  dater <- unique(obs)
  datex <- c(1: length(unique(obs)))
  #boop <- filter(both_numbered_sorted, both_numbered_sorted$date == datex [2])
  
  #loop to create a separate dataframe for each bird that only contains interactions #pertaining to that bird.
  #Each dataframe is stored within a list to make the dataframes easy to work with
  #separate data into list of each obs session each day 
  for(i in datex) { 
    output <- filter(pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand, pass_filt_binded_2022G1_rand$date_period == dater[i] )
    if (nrow(output) < 1) {output [ nrow(output) + 1 , ] <- NA
    } 
    pass_side_by_side_tested_time_2022G1_list[[paste0("list", dater[i])]] <- output}
  
  
  

nruns <- 1000

pb = txtProgressBar(min=0, max = nruns, style = 3)

#run ids should be 1 through however many times you want to run through the permutation
runIDs <- seq(1,1000)

#create an empty masterframe to be filled with each run of the permutation
masterframe_passalong.df_time_2022G1 <- data.frame(runIDs = character(),
                             actor = character(),
                             actor_rank = numeric(),
                             subject = character(),
                             subject_rank = numeric(),
                             date = character(),
                             full_time = integer(),
                             bin = integer(),
                             behavior = character(),
                             type = character(),                                        
                             phase = character(),
                             counter = character(),
                             actor_2 = character(),
                             actor_2_rank = numeric(),
                             subject_2 = character(),
                             subject_2_rank = numeric(),
                             full_time_2 = integer(),
                             date2 = character(),
                             initiated = character(),
                             rule_followed = character(),
                             time_to_pass = integer(),
                            date_period = character(),
                             stringsAsFactors=FALSE)


set.seed(18)
#forloop starts here'
for (r in 1:length(runIDs)) { #r = 1 is a test you can uncomment to see if it works before #running it 100 times
#r = 1
  run <- r
      setTxtProgressBar(pb, r) #update progress bar
  loop.run <- paste("run", run) 
  loop.run
  #for example, if r = 1, looprun will say run 1 which will allow you to keep each run's #data straight
  
    

  
  #renamed data
  loop.datalist <- pass_side_by_side_tested_time_2022G1_list
    head(loop.datalist[[3]])
    xorder <- c()
  listlength <- as.numeric(length(loop.datalist))
datex2 <- 1:listlength
  full_time_to_rule <-c()
  loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added <- c()
    
    

  for(i in datex2) {
         #store time
     full_time_to_rule[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = c(time_to_pass))     
     #remove time from each df in the list. randomizing the order of time wouldnt make sense
     loop.datalist[[i]] <- subset(loop.datalist[[i]], select = -c(time_to_pass))
      #create a randomized sequence of numbers to bind to the subset data (ex. 2,6,1,3,5,4)
      #sort the data frame to put the randomized numbers in order from 1 to whatever and voila, the time between events (speed) has been  randomized (ex. from above 1,2,3,4,5,6)
    xorder[[i]] <- data.frame(xorder = sample(1:length(full_time_to_rule[[i]]$time_to_pass),
                                              length(full_time_to_rule[[i]]$time_to_pass)))
    #bind random order to df
    full_time_to_rule[[i]] <- mutate(full_time_to_rule[[i]], 
                                 xorderx = xorder[[i]]$xorder)
    #put randomized order of numbers in order to randomize speed in the dataset
    full_time_to_rule[[i]] <-
      full_time_to_rule[[i]][order(full_time_to_rule[[i]]$xorderx),]
    #bind time back on
    loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added[[i]] <- bind_cols(loop.datalist[[i]], full_time_to_rule[[i]])
    loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added[[i]] <- subset(loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added[[i]], select = -c(xorderx))

   }
  

  
  
  
  
  
  
  

#combine dataframes into one huge dataframe
loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_pass_2022G1 <- bind_rows(loop.data.randomized.time_to_rule.added, .id = "column_label")


 

#sort by time and date
loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_pass_2022G1 <- loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_pass_2022G1 %>%
  arrange(loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_pass_2022G1$date, loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_pass_2022G1$full_time)






#Remove duplicate events, events that have been represented twice, and events that could not confidently be counted as the start of passalong
testhugesorted_no_dup_rand_time <- subset(loop.datalist_time_randomized_binded_tested_pass_2022G1, initiated != "no") 

passalong_no_dup_rand_time <- subset(testhugesorted_no_dup_rand_time, select = c(date, full_time, actor, actor_rank, subject, subject_rank,
                                                               counter, bin, behavior, type, phase, actor_2, actor_2_rank, subject_2, subject_2_rank, full_time_2, date2, initiated,  rule_followed,time_to_pass, date_period))





  
  #end of passalong process
  #end of rule code
  
  
  
  #bind vector of run IDs for each run to the dataframe so that you can have a numeric run #id next to each data point
  #that coresponds with that run which will allow you to sort/put them in ascending order later
 runIDs <- rep(loop.run, length(passalong_no_dup_rand_time$actor))
 
  passalong_complete_rand_time_2022G1 <- cbind(runIDs, passalong_no_dup_rand_time)
  
  
#put each runs data into teh masterframe
  masterframe_passalong.df_time_2022G1 <- rbind(masterframe_passalong.df_time_2022G1, passalong_complete_rand_time_2022G1)
  
}



pass_time_length_list_masterframe_2022G1[[paste0("bin", k)]] <- masterframe_passalong.df_time_2022G1
}

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded <-  bind_rows(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_2022G1, .id = "column_label")


end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")
start_time
end_time



#write.csv(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded,  here("ch3 data","pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded.csv"), row.names = FALSE)






```



#load df
```{r}


#pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded <- read_csv(here("ch3 data","pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded.csv"))


head(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded) 


```
#bin2
```{r}
#bin2

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin2 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 2)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin2 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 2)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin2 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin2, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin2 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin2, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin2)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin2 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin2, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin2$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin2$time_to_pass)


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin2 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin2, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))




#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin2 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin2, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin2, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin2 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin2 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin2

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 43.9403
#rand 444.9257


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin2 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period) , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin2 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin2)

#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin2 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin2, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin2

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin2, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin2)


```

#bin4
```{r}
#bin4

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin4 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 4)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin4 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 4)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin4 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin4, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin4 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin4, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))

#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin4 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin4, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin4$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin4$time_to_pass)


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin4 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin4, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin4 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin4, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin4, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin4 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin4 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin4

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 43.9403
#rand 444.9257


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin4 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin4 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin4)

#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin4 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin4, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin4

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin4, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin4)


```


#bin5
```{r}
#bin5

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin5 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 5)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin5 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 5)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin5 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin5, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin5 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin5, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin5)

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin5$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin5$time_to_pass)

#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df

  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes

   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
```

```{r}
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 120.0000
#rand 301.0888


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin6 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)    , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin6 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin6)
#p = 0.0131 
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin6 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin6, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin6
#(Intercept)         3.870  5.522
#data_typerandomized 0.203  1.817




confint_pass_2022G1_bin6



#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin6, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin6)
#0.0005157713 0.04206279
```

```{r}
#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin8)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin8 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin8, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin8

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin8 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 27.000
#rand 954.752


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin8 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period) , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin8 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin8)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin8 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin8, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin8

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin8, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin8)


```


#bin9
```{r}
#bin9

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin9 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 9)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin9 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 9)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin9 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin9, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin9 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin9, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin9)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin9 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin9, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin9$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin9$time_to_pass)



pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin9 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin9, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin9 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin9, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin9 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin9, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin9, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin9 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin9 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .9, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin9

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin9 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period) + (1|actor_2) , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin9 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin9)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin9 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin9, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin9

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized

summary(glht(m1pass_bin9, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin9)
```

```{r}
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin12)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin12 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin12, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin12

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin12 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin12 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin12 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin12)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin12 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin12, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin12

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin12, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin12)


```



#bin13
```{r}
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin13 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 13)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin13 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 13)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin13 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin13, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin13 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin13, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin13)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin13 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin13, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin13$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin13$time_to_pass)



pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin13 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin13, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin13 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin13, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin13 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin13, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin13, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin13 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin13 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin13

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin13 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin13 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin13)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin13 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin13, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized

summary(glht(m1pass_bin13, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin13)
```

```{r}
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin16)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin16 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin16, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin16

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin16 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin16 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin16 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin16)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin16 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin16, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin16

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin16, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin16)


```




#bin17
```{r}
#bin17

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin17 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 17)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin17 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 17)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin17 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin17, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin17 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin17, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin17)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin17 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin17, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin17$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin17$time_to_pass)



pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin17 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin17, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin17 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin17, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin17 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin17, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin17, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin17 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin17 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin17

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin17 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin17 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin17)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin17 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin17, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin17

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin17, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin17)
```

```{r}
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin20)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin20 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin20, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin20

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin20 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin20 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin20 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin20)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin20 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin20, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin20

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin20, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin20)

```


#bin21
```{r}
#bin21

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin21 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 21)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin21 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 21)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin21 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin21, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin21 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin21, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin21)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin21 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin21, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin21$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin21$time_to_pass)



pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin21 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin21, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin21 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin21, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin21 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin21, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin21, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin21 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin21 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin21

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin21 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period)  , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin21 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin21)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin21 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin21, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin21

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin21, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin21)
```

```{r}
#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin23 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 614.5714
#rand 614.5714


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin23 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period) , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin23 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin23)
#p = 0.411    
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin23 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin23, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin23
#(Intercept)          5.961  7.424
#data_typerandomized -0.373  0.908

#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin23, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin23)

#2.861875e-05 0.20183510
```

```{r}
#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin24)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin24 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin24, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin24

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin24 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1
#obs 
#rand 


#linear mixed model to compare means of observed vs randomized rule speed

m1pass_bin24 <- glmer(time_to_pass ~ data_type  + (1 | date_period) + (1|actor_2) , data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin24 ,   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)), family = Gamma(link = "log"))
summary(m1pass_bin24)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals  
confint_pass_2022G1_bin24 <- round(confint(m1pass_bin24, method = "Wald", level = 0.95), 3)
confint_pass_2022G1_bin24

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized 
#tukey
summary(glht(m1pass_bin24, linfct = mcp(data_type="Tukey")), test = adjusted("none"))
library(MuMIn)
#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin24)

```


#bin25
```{r}
#bin25

pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin25 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded, bin == 25)

pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin25 <- subset(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1, bin == 25)



#subset masterframe so that only rule follows are included
pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin25 <- subset(pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_binded_bin25, rule_followed == "yes" )

#isolate time it takes to carry out rule...
masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin25 <- subset( pass_time_length_list_masterframe_st_unst_2022G1_only_follows_bin25, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))
head(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin25)



#add data label for the randomized rule speed
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin25 <- mutate(masterframe_pass_time_sub.df_2022G1_bin25, data_type = "randomized")

#ensure that time is numeric
masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin25$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin25$time_to_pass)



pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin25 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin25, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))


pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin25 <- subset( pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin25, select = c(date, full_time, actor, subject, 
                                                                         actor_2, subject_2, full_time_2, date2, 
                                                                         initiated, rule_followed, time_to_pass, date_period, bin, behavior, type, phase))



#add data label for the observed rule speed
pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin25 <- mutate(pass_time_length_st_unst_2022G1_real_bin25, data_type = "observed")


#combine df for observed rule speed and randomized rule speed into one df
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25 <- rbind(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df_bin25, pass_length_ready_2022G1_label_bin25 )
unique(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25$data_type)




#provide label for rule in question
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25 <- mutate(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25, rule = "pass")


#make sure that rule speed is numeric
pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25$time_to_pass <- as.numeric(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25$time_to_pass)


#observed vs randomized raw plot. quick look at how the distriubtuions compare
ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25, aes(x=time_to_pass, fill = data_type)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..),      
                 binwidth=1) +#,
                 #colour="black", fill="white") + 
  geom_density() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "length in s of pass events random vs observed", limits = c(0,300)) 


#obtain mode of observed and randomized rule speed for plotting purposes
#getmode <- function(v) {
   #uniqv <- unique(v)
   #uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
#}

#pass_2022G1_mode_obs <- getmode(pass_length_ready_2022G1_label$time_to_pass)

#pass_2022G1_mode_rand <-getmode(masterframe_pass_time_ready.df$time_to_pass)

#bind mode to dfs
#pass_2022G1_mode <-  rbind(pass_2022G1_mode_obs,pass_2022G1_mode_rand)

#test for homogeneity of variance
#leveneTest(time_to_pass ~ data_type, data = pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25)


#split violin plot of observed vs randomized rule speed
pass_violin_plot_bin25 <- ggplot(pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25, aes(rule, time_to_pass, fill = data_type))  +
  introdataviz::geom_split_violin(alpha= .10, trim = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .2, alpha = .2, fatten = 2, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA) +
  #stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "point",  colour = "darkblue", show.legend = F, 
               #position = position_dodge(.200))  +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Time Dependent Rule") +  
     coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Speed of pass events (s)",
                     breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200) 
                     ) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("red","gray")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20)  + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=25), axis.title= element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())



pass_violin_plot_bin25

#calculate the means for observed and randomized rule speed
mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1 <- pass_length_ready_2022G1_comb_bin25 %>% group_by(data_type) %>%
  dplyr::summarize (mean(time_to_pass))

mean_obs_rand_pass_2022G1

summary(m1pass_bin25)

#only 1 pass
#calculate confidence intervals

confint_pass_2022G1_bin25

#(Intercept)         
#data_typerandomized

#find R2 value
r.squaredGLMM(m1pass_bin25)
```
